req.title: Manager updates employee
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to update a employee through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: This test suite validates the manager employee update workflow across three critical paths: (1) happy path with valid mandatory fields to verify successful update and audit logging, (2) missing mandatory field to verify 400 validation error, and (3) boundary conditions on field lengths and special characters. The suite applies Equivalence Partitioning to group valid/invalid inputs, Boundary Value Analysis to test field length limits, and Negative Path testing for authentication and authorization failures. All tests are deterministic, self-contained, and traceable to requirement outcomes.

tests.name: test_manager_updates_employee_success
actions: Query audit log for employee_id=42 and action=UPDATE
expected: Audit log entry exists with timestamp, user_id, action=UPDATE, resource_type=employee
trace: R-WEB-002: response 200 or 201 with entity id

tests.name: test_manager_updates_employee_missing_mandatory_field
preconditions: first_name, last_name, email are mandatory fields
actions: POST /employees/42 with incomplete payload: {"first_name": "John"} (missing last_name and email)
Capture response status and error message
expected: Response body contains validation error details for missing fields
No audit log entry created for this request
trace: R-WEB-002: mandatory fields must be validated

tests.name: test_manager_updates_employee_unauthenticated
preconditions: User session is invalid or token expired
tests.executable:
def test_manager_updates_employee_unauthenticated(client, audit_db):
    emp_id = 42
    payload = {"first_name": "Jane", "last_name": "Smith", "email": "jane@example.com"}
    resp = client.post(f"/employees/{emp_id}", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code == 401
    audit_count = audit_db.count_documents({"employee_id": emp_id})
    assert audit_count == 0

tests.name: test_manager_updates_employee_forbidden_role
test_manager_updates_employee_boundary_max_field_length
preconditions: first_name and last_name have max length constraint (e.g. 100 chars)
actions: POST /employees/42 with first_name at boundary (100 chars exactly)
POST /employees/42 with first_name exceeding boundary (101 chars)
expected: First request (100 chars) returns 200 or 201 with employee updated
Second request (101 chars) returns 400 with field length error
trace: R-WEB-002: mandatory fields must be validated
BVA: test at exact boundary and just beyond
tests.executable:
def test_manager_updates_employee_boundary_max_field_length(auth_client, audit_db):
    emp_id = 42
    name_100 = "A" * 100
    name_101 = "A" * 101
    resp1 = auth_client.post(f"/employees/{emp_id}", json={
        "first_name": name_100,
        "last_name": "Doe",
        "email": "john@example.com"
    })
    assert resp1.status_code in [200, 201]
    resp2 = auth_client.post(f"/employees/{emp_id}", json={
        "first_name": name_101,
        "last_name": "Doe",
        "email": "john@example.com"
    })
    assert resp2.status_code == 400
    audits = list(audit_db.find({"employee_id": emp_id, "action": "UPDATE"}))
    assert len(audits) == 1

tests.name: test_manager_updates_employee_special_characters_in_name
preconditions: System accepts names with hyphens, apostrophes, accents
actions: Capture response status and returned name values
expected: Returned employee data preserves special characters exactly
trace: BVA: boundary test with special characters
tests.executable:
def test_manager_updates_employee_special_characters_in_name(auth_client, audit_db):
    emp_id = 42
    payload = {
        "first_name": "Jean-Pierre",
        "last_name": "O'Brien",
        "email": "jean@example.com"
    }
    resp = auth_client.post(f"/employees/{emp_id}", json=payload)
    assert resp.status_code in [200, 201]
    body = resp.json()
    assert body["first_name"] == "Jean-Pierre"
    assert body["last_name"] == "O'Brien"
    audit_entry = audit_db.find_one({"employee_id": emp_id, "action": "UPDATE"})
    assert audit_entry is not None

tests.name: test_manager_updates_employee_invalid_email_format
actions: Capture response status and validation error
expected: Error message references email format validation
trace: R-WEB-002: mandatory fields must be validated
